description: Chillist plan creation wizard tag schema. Tier 1 = plan archetype (single-select). Universal flags asked once regardless of Tier 1. Tier 2 = named axes conditional on Tier 1, each axis internally single-select to prevent contradictions. Tier 3 = specifics conditional on Tier 2 values.

structural_contract.purpose: Structural guarantees that both consumers (frontend wizard + chatbot service) rely on. Changes to data INSIDE these structures are safe and flow through automatically. Changes to the structure ITSELF require coordinated updates to consumer code and bot instructions.
guarantees: tier1 is always an object with 'select' (always 'single') and 'options' (array of {id, label, emoji}).

universal_flags is an object mapping flag names to flag definitions. Each flag has: 'key', 'select' ('single' | 'multi'), 'required' (boolean), 'options' (array). Multi-select flags may include 'max_select'.

tier2_axes is an object mapping axis names to axis definitions. Each axis has: 'key', 'select' ('single' | 'multi'), 'shown_for_tier1' (array of tier1 ids), 'options' (array of {id, label}). Single-select axes may include 'defaults_by_tier1' and 'hidden_options_by_tier1'.
tier3.options_by_parent maps any tier2 option id to an array of {id, label} drill-down options. Absence means no drill-down for that option.
item_generation_bundles maps a bundle name to an array of item strings. Universal flag options reference bundles via 'injects_bundle'.
All ids are stable strings safe to use as database values. Renaming an id is a breaking change; renaming a label is not.
safe_changes_no_bot_update_needed: Adding, removing, or renaming labels/emojis on existing options
Adding new options to tier1, universal_flags, tier2_axes, or tier3
Changing which tier1 ids appear in 'shown_for_tier1' for an axis
breaking_changes_bot_update_required: Adding a new top-level key (e.g. introducing tier4)
Adding a new tier2 axis (bot must decide ordering and default-fill behavior)
Adding a new universal_flag (bot must decide where in the conversation it's asked)
Changing the 'select' rule of an existing axis (single ↔ multi)
Renaming any id (breaks stored data and bot extraction prompts)
Changing the meaning/purpose of an existing field

design_principles: Wizard only captures what the owner knows at creation time, isn't derivable later (from dates/location/participants), and materially changes the item list.
Each Tier 2 axis is internally single-select (mutex) to structurally prevent contradictions like 'cooking' + 'eating out'. 'Mix' is its own value, not two checkboxes.
Activities axis is the only multi-select axis in Tier 2.
No 'Other' Tier 1 — bounce rate signals which archetype is missing for v1.2.
Group composition (kids, pets, dietary) and weather/season are intentionally excluded — derived later from participants and dates.

tier1.label: What kind of plan is this?

destination_scope.label: Where is it?
destination_scope.notes: When 'abroad' is chosen, item generator injects the travel_abroad bundle: passport, visa check, travel insurance, power adapter, local SIM/eSIM, currency, vaccination docs.

group_character.label: How would you describe the group? (optional, pick up to 3)
group_character.purpose: Feeds the LLM item-generation prompt. Each trait demonstrably shifts the item list.

tier2_axes.description: Each axis is a named question with its own select rule. Axes are conditionally shown based on Tier 1. Each non-multi axis is internally mutex.

options.label: Mix of cooking and eating out

activities.label: What will you do? (pick any that apply)

tier3.description: Specifics conditional on Tier 2 axis values. Keyed by Tier 2 option id.

mixed_food.label: Mostly cooking breakfast, eating out otherwise
Mostly cooking dinner, eating out otherwise

item_generation_bundles.description: Pre-defined item bundles injected by specific flag values. Keeps the LLM prompt smaller and ensures critical items are never missed.

1.1: Restructured Tier 2 from flat per-Tier-1 lists into named axes (sleep, food_strategy, activities, venue) — each internally single-select to prevent contradictions.
Split Tier 1 'hotel_trip' into 'hotel_stay' and 'airbnb_stay' (different food/cooking item profiles).
Promoted 'games_gathering' and 'party' to Tier 1 (previously buried as dinner sub-flavors).
Removed Tier 1 'Other' — absence is a deliberate v1.2 research signal.
Added universal flag 'destination_scope' (domestic / abroad) — abroad injects travel bundle.
Added universal flag 'group_character' — optional multi-select, max 3, feeds LLM prompt.
Added per-Tier-1 defaults for sleep and food_strategy axes for sensible pre-selection.
Added hidden_options_by_tier1 for food_strategy to suppress nonsensical combinations (e.g. 'all-inclusive' under 'dinner').
Excluded weather/season (derived from date+location), group composition and dietary (from participants), duration (from dates).
